body_txt: Basically a character dressing in a manner that just doesn't fit the cultural norm for the character's age. It could be in clothing, hairstyle, makeup, or something else. The reasons can vary, although dressing up or costumes wouldn't count for this. This is more a character either not realizing how they look dressed like that, or maybe even trying to pretend they are a different age (either as a disguise, or as a denial about one's age). Examples of "too young" from Japan in the under-18 range are often subject to Values Dissonance , as the legal age of consent is currently 16 in the country, and was only 13 until 2023. It's not at all uncommon for such characters to be aged-up in localizations, since what would scarcely raise an eyebrow in Japan is seen as highly creepy and ephebophilic to Western audiences. This can be justified when characters are Older Than They Look or Younger Than They Look , and dressing their actual age would look like this trope even though it isn't. A Super-Trope to Adults Dressed as Children . Compare Mistaken Age , Improbable Age , Dawson Casting and Age-Inappropriate Art . See also No Dress Code , I Was Quite a Fashion Victim . A common cause for a Parental Fashion Veto .
